title: Boiling point of water (direct route)
query: What is the boiling point of water in Celsius at sea level?
expected_criteria: States 100°C (or equivalent) as the answer
Concise, direct answer with no unnecessary research framing or hedging
Does not cite external sources for a fact this basic
notes: Live-verified via orchestrator.decide(): resolves to route=direct. Baseline anchor for the simplest possible direct-route case.

title: API acronym definition (direct route)
expected_criteria: Correctly expands to Application Programming Interface
Brief, no unnecessary research framing

title: Ambiguous account help request (clarify route)
query: Can you help me with my account?
expected_criteria: Asks a clarifying question rather than guessing what kind of help is needed
Does not fabricate account details or assume a specific service/platform
Identifies what's ambiguous (which account, what kind of help)
notes: Live-verified: route=clarify. No prior conversation context, so the request is genuinely underspecified.

title: Vague continuation with no context (clarify route)
query: Can you go deeper on that?
expected_criteria: Asks what 'that' refers to, since there is no prior conversation context
Does not guess at a topic and answer anyway
notes: Live-verified: route=clarify. Tests that a referential/continuation-shaped query without prior turns correctly triggers clarification instead of hallucinating context.

title: Freshness signal redirects direct question to research
query: What are the newest features announced in the latest iOS release?
expected_criteria: Cites a dated/sourced answer rather than relying on stale training-data recall
References official Apple sources or recent news coverage
Does not present outdated iOS version info as current
notes: Live-verified: route=research (research_level=easy). Tests routing_policy.evaluate_routing_signals' freshness-signal redirect — a 'newest/latest' query should force research over a stale direct-from-training answer even though it superficially looks like a simple question.

title: Business memo formatted as a document (document route)
query: Write a one-page business memo announcing our new return policy, formatted as a downloadable document.
expected_criteria: Produces a structured, one-page memo
Professional business-memo tone and formatting
Does not require external research/sourcing
notes: Live-verified: route=document, output_format=docx. No research needed — pure composition/formatting intent.

title: Cover letter as a Word document (document route)
query: Write a cover letter for a marketing manager position and give it to me as a Word document.
expected_criteria: Produces a complete, usable cover letter
Addresses a marketing manager role specifically
Delivered/described as a document artifact, not just chat text

title: Research-backed comparison document (research_document route)
query: Research the top 3 CRM platforms for small businesses and put together a one-page comparison document I can share with my team.
expected_criteria: Includes researched, sourced comparison data for at least 3 named CRM platforms
Delivered as a structured document artifact, not a plain chat answer
Comparison is organized (e.g. table or clear sections), not just prose
notes: Live-verified: route=research_document. Tests the combined research+document path — distinct from both 'research' (no artifact) and 'document' (no sourcing).

title: High-stakes investment term triggers deep tier
query: Conduct a thorough investment risk analysis comparing three publicly traded REIT funds.
expected_criteria: Treats this as deep research given the 'investment'/'thorough' high-stakes framing
Covers risk analysis across at least 3 named REIT funds
Cites financial data sources, not just general knowledge
notes: Live-verified: route=research, research_level=deep, requires_confirmation=true. Tests choose_research_level()'s high_stakes_terms keyword path ('investment', 'thorough') as a distinct deep-research trigger from the multi-subject+dimension signal tested by the cloud-storage and privacy-regulation anchor cases. This case also exercises deep_research_gate (the confirmation-gate two-pass check).

title: Simple current-fact lookup needing freshness (regular/easy tier research)
query: What is the current federal funds interest rate set by the Federal Reserve?
expected_criteria: Cites a current, sourced figure rather than a stale or guessed number
References the Federal Reserve as the authoritative source
Does not over-elaborate with unnecessary multi-dimension research depth
notes: Live-verified: route=research, research_level=easy. Single-subject factual lookup needing freshness but not deep multi-dimension research — tests the low end of the tier spectrum, distinct from the deep-tier anchor cases.

title: Quick current-conditions lookup (research, low tier)
query: What's the weather like in Tokyo right now?
expected_criteria: Provides current weather conditions, not a generic/seasonal answer
Cites a live/current source
Concise — does not over-research a simple lookup
notes: Live-verified: route=research, research_level=regular. Another low-tier research anchor — needs live data so can't be 'direct', but doesn't warrant deep multi-dimension treatment.

title: Multi-jurisdiction privacy regulation comparison (second deep-tier anchor)
query: Compare data privacy regulations between GDPR, CCPA, and PIPEDA across consent requirements, breach notification timelines, and enforcement penalties.
expected_criteria: Classifies as deep research (3 subjects x 3 dimensions, same structural shape as the cloud-storage anchor case)
Covers GDPR, CCPA, and PIPEDA individually across all three dimensions
Cites official regulatory text or government sources for each
Does not synthesize a single 'best' regulation or rank them — descriptive comparison only
notes: Live-verified: route=research, research_level=deep, requires_confirmation=true. A second dimension-richness deep-tier anchor case outside the cloud/tech domain, to diversify deep_research_gate and subject-coverage-balancing test coverage (see #23, #26) beyond the existing cloud_storage_no_recommendation_multi_dimension case.
